name: Greater Memphis Financial Empowerment Center
description_short: Non-profit financial counseling center in Memphis offering free certified financial counselor services and credit education for Shelby County residents.
description_long:
Greater Memphis Financial Empowerment Center (GMFEC) is a resource center designed to serve Shelby County citizens in need of financial counseling and education. The organization operates a physical office located at 1355 Lynnfield Rd, Bldg B, Ste 101 in East Memphis, with support from RISE Foundation, which provides on-site operations management. The center is staffed by certified financial counselors and administrative personnel dedicated to helping consumers improve their financial situations.

GMFEC offers free financial counseling sessions by appointment, available Monday-Wednesday 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM and Tuesday-Thursday 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM. The center provides flexible service delivery through both in-office appointments at their main location and mobile counseling at multiple co-location partner sites throughout Shelby County. Consumers can schedule appointments online through their appointment system or by calling 901-390-4200. The organization takes a client-centered approach by offering to arrange appointments at convenient county locations if clients cannot travel to the main office.

What distinguishes GMFEC is its integration with community partners and its emphasis on accessibility for underserved populations in the Memphis area. The use of certified financial counselors (likely NFCC-certified based on the non-profit structure) and the partnership model with RISE Foundation suggests professional-grade guidance. The willingness to conduct mobile counseling at partner sites demonstrates commitment to removing barriers to service access across Shelby County.

This organization appears to be a legitimate, professionally-managed non-profit financial counseling service. The clear contact information, defined hours, appointment system, and partnership structure all indicate established operations. However, the website provides limited detail about specific service offerings beyond general financial counseling, and there is no information about specializations, success rates, or specific topics addressed in sessions.
pros: Free financial counseling services — no cost to clients
Certified financial counselors on staff providing professional guidance
Flexible scheduling with extended hours (9 AM - 7 PM on some days)
Mobile counseling available at multiple co-location partner sites throughout Shelby County
Online appointment scheduling system for convenience
Supported by RISE Foundation with dedicated on-site operations manager
No mention of fees, deposits, or hidden charges
cons: Website provides minimal detail about specific services or counseling topics covered
Limited information about counselor credentials or specializations
Appointment-only model may create scheduling barriers for some clients
Website content is sparse and does not clearly explain what financial counseling includes
No publicly available success stories or client testimonials accessible from contact page
best_for: Shelby County residents seeking free, professional financial counseling
Consumers needing general financial guidance and education
Individuals who prefer in-person counseling at accessible local locations
Community members unable to afford paid financial planning services
diagnosis: GMFEC is best for Shelby County residents seeking free, professional financial counseling from certified advisors without cost barriers. The main caveat is that the website provides insufficient detail about specific service offerings, making it difficult for potential clients to understand whether the counseling covers credit repair, debt management, budgeting, homeownership, or other specializations.
services: In-office appointments at main Memphis location
Mobile counseling at partner co-location sites
